TMPDIR can be unset/unsafe, so $TMPDIR/foo may expand to /foo at runtime. Remove TMPDIR allowlist to match python guard behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T08:10:30.830349Z","updated_at":"2025-12-29T08:15:13.987974Z","closed_at":"2025-12-29T08:15:13.987974Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-o85","title":"Task: Port agent lessons (4-6) to webapp","description":"# Port Agent Lessons (4-6) to Webapp

## Parent Feature
Lesson Content Pages (cxk)

## Scope
Create lesson pages for the agent-focused lessons:
- Lesson 4: Agent Commands (`04_agents_login.md`)
- Lesson 5: NTM Command Center (`05_ntm_core.md`)
- Lesson 6: NTM Prompt Palette (`06_ntm_command_palette.md`)

## Why Grouped
These lessons are interconnected:
- All focus on using the AI coding agents
- Build on each other (auth → orchestration → prompts)
- Share visual elements (command tables, prompt examples)

## Lesson-Specific Enhancements

### Lesson 4: Agent Commands
This is a critical lesson teaching users to authenticate with agents.

Enhancements:
- Interactive auth status checker component
- Clear distinction between ChatGPT OAuth vs API keys
- CAAM backup/restore workflow diagram
- Warning callouts for common mistakes

Key Content:
```markdown
| Agent | Command | Alias | Company |
|-------|---------|-------|---------|
| Claude Code | `claude` | `cc` | Anthropic |
| Codex CLI | `codex` | `cod` | OpenAI |
| Gemini CLI | `gemini` | `gmi` | Google |
```

### Lesson 5: NTM Command Center
Introduces the core orchestration tool.

Enhancements:
- Visual session management diagram
- `ntm spawn` command builder (optional interactive)
- Session lifecycle visualization

Key Content:
- Session types and naming
- Spawning agents
- Attaching and detaching
- Sending prompts

### Lesson 6: NTM Prompt Palette
Teaches pre-built prompts and customization.

Enhancements:
- Searchable prompt gallery
- Copy-to-clipboard for prompts
- Category filtering (research, coding, review)

## Visual Components Needed
1. **AgentCard** - Shows agent status (authenticated/not)
2. **PromptCard** - Copyable prompt with syntax highlighting
3. **SessionDiagram** - Visual representation of ntm sessions

## Cross-Links
- Link to wizard Step 7 (Set Up Accounts) for auth setup
- Link to Reference Docs for command details
- Link to Lesson 7 (Flywheel) for full workflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 3 agent lessons accessible
- [ ] Agent tables render correctly
- [ ] Command examples have copy buttons
- [ ] Warning callouts display prominently
- [ ] Prompt examples are copyable
- [ ] Mobile-friendly layout

## Dependencies
Same as foundation lessons:
- Lesson progress tracking (ori)
- MDX rendering pipeline (25i)
- Lesson layout and navigation (j9a)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:21:18.624349Z","updated_at":"2025-12-21T23:26:23.584372Z","closed_at":"2025-12-21T23:26:23.584372Z","close_reason":"All 9 lessons already load and render from source markdown files via lesson-content.tsx.  {"id":"bd-o86r","title":"useVPSIP should store trimmed IP in state","description":"apps/web/lib/userPreferences.ts: useVPSIP() updates state with the raw input string even though setVPSIP() normalizes/trim()s before persisting. This can leave leading/trailing spaces in React state/UI even though stored value is normalized.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T08:48:44.196823Z","updated_at":"2025-12-29T08:49:10.648766Z","closed_at":"2025-12-29T08:49:10.648766Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-o8dq6","title":"Make Gemini CLI patcher tolerant when real node is absent","description":"ACFS installer still emits a non-fatal warning during coding-agent install when the Gemini CLI patch helper requires a real node binary and only bun is present.

Observed in Docker validation on Ubuntu 24.04 during install phase:
- Gemini CLI patch step failed: node is required but not found (bun's node wrapper is not compatible)
- Installer continues successfully and full update E2E passes

Follow-up should either:
1. make the patcher degrade quietly when prerequisites are absent, or
2. ensure ACFS installs/provides a compatible node runtime before invoking the patcher.

Validation context: all current ACFS fixes for doctor/update/workspace/self-update are complete; this is residual cleanup only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-08T17:48:32.194544100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T17:48:32.194544100Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- Installer continues successfully and full update E2E passes

Follow-up should either:
1. make the patcher degrade quietly when prerequisites are absent, or
2. ensure ACFS installs/provides a compatible node runtime before invoking the patcher.

 {"id":"bd-o9dh","title":"Integration testing for newproj TUI wizard","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end (E2E) testing of the complete TUI wizard flow to ensure it works correctly across different scenarios, terminal types, and edge cases. This bead focuses on INTEGRATION testing - testing the wizard as a whole from user's perspective.

**Note:** Unit tests for individual functions are handled separately in the unit testing framework bead (kvob).

## Test Framework

### Recommended: expect + bats
- **expect**: Automate interactive TUI sessions (send keystrokes, read output)
- **bats**: Structure E2E tests as test suites with assertions
- **Alternative**: pexpect (Python) if more complex automation needed

### Test Script Structure
```
tests/
├── unit/                          # Unit tests (separate bead)
├── e2e/
│   ├── test_helper.bash           # E2E-specific helpers
│   ├── expect_helpers/            # Reusable expect scripts
│   │   ├── complete_wizard.exp    # Full happy path
│   │   ├── cancel_wizard.exp      # Test cancellation
│   │   └── navigate_back.exp      # Test back navigation
│   ├── test_happy_path.bats       # Happy path E2E tests
│   ├── test_cancellation.bats     # Interrupt/cancel tests
│   ├── test_validation.bats       # Input validation tests
│   ├── test_recovery.bats         # Error recovery tests
│   └── test_terminal_compat.bats  # Terminal compatibility
└── e2e_logs/                      # Detailed E2E test logs
    └── YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_<test>.log
```

## E2E Test Categories

### 1. Happy Path Tests
- Complete wizard flow with all defaults
- Complete wizard with all customizations
- Verify created file contents match expectations
- Verify AGENTS.md sections based on detected tech stack

```bash
@test "E2E: complete wizard with defaults creates expected structure" {
    local tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    run expect tests/e2e/expect_helpers/complete_wizard.exp "$tmpdir" "test-project"
    assert_success
    
    # Verify structure
    assert [ -d "$tmpdir/test-project/.git" ]
    assert [ -f "$tmpdir/test-project/AGENTS.md" ]
    assert [ -f "$tmpdir/test-project/.gitignore" ]
    assert [ -f "$tmpdir/test-project/.ubsignore" ]
    
    rm -rf "$tmpdir"
}
```

### 2. Terminal Compatibility Tests
- Test on standard terminal sizes (80x24, 120x40)
- Test with different TERM values (xterm-256color, screen, tmux, dumb)
- Test color rendering fallback (no color support)
- Test without gum installed (fallback mode)

```bash
@test "E2E: wizard works with TERM=dumb" {
    export TERM=dumb
    run expect tests/e2e/expect_helpers/complete_wizard.exp ...
    assert_success
    # Verify no ANSI escape codes in output
}
```

### 3. Navigation Tests
- Back navigation preserves all state
- Escape shows cancellation confirmation
- Enter accepts defaults correctly
- Tab focus order is logical

```bash
@test "E2E: back navigation preserves state" {
    run expect tests/e2e/expect_helpers/navigate_back.exp
    assert_success
    assert_output --partial "Previous value restored"
}
```

### 4. Error Recovery Tests
- Wizard recovers from failed directory creation
- Wizard handles Ctrl+C gracefully
- Wizard cleans up partial state on failure
- Wizard shows helpful error messages

```bash
@test "E2E: Ctrl+C during creation rolls back" {
    local tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    chmod 000 "$tmpdir"  # Make unwritable
    
    run timeout 10 expect tests/e2e/expect_helpers/create_in_readonly.exp "$tmpdir"
    
    # Should have cleaned up
    assert [ ! -d "$tmpdir/test-project" ]
    
    chmod 755 "$tmpdir"
    rm -rf "$tmpdir"
}
```

### 5. Edge Case Tests
- Empty directory (no tech stack detected)
- Monorepo with multiple tech stacks detected
- Very long project name (truncation)
- Project name with edge characters (underscores, numbers)

### 6. Integration with ACFS Tools
- Created project passes `shellcheck` on any generated shell files
- `bd` initializes correctly (if available)
- `ubs .` runs without errors on created project
- Claude Code recognizes .claude/settings.toml

```bash
@test "E2E: created project passes shellcheck" {
    local tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    # ... run wizard ...
    
    # Find all shell files and check them
    local shell_files=$(find "$tmpdir/test-project" -name "*.sh" -o -name "*.bash")
    for f in $shell_files; do
        run shellcheck "$f"
        assert_success
    done
}
```

## Detailed Logging for E2E

### Log Everything
```bash
# In test_helper.bash
setup() {
    E2E_LOG_DIR="${BATS_TEST_DIRNAME}/../e2e_logs"
    mkdir -p "$E2E_LOG_DIR"
    
    E2E_TEST_LOG="$E2E_LOG_DIR/$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)_${BATS_TEST_NAME}.log"
    
    # Header
    {
        echo "========================================"
        echo "E2E Test: ${BATS_TEST_NAME}"
        echo "Started: $(date -Iseconds)"
        echo "========================================"
    } >> "$E2E_TEST_LOG"
}

# Log function for E2E tests
e2e_log() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S.%3N)] [$level] $*" >> "$E2E_TEST_LOG"
}

# Capture expect session output
run_expect_logged() {
    local expect_script="$1"
    shift
    
    e2e_log "INFO" "Running expect: $expect_script $*"
    
    local output_file=$(mktemp)
    if expect "$expect_script" "$@" > "$output_file" 2>&1; then
        e2e_log "PASS" "expect succeeded"
        cat "$output_file" >> "$E2E_TEST_LOG"
        cat "$output_file"
        rm "$output_file"
        return 0
    else
        local errno=$?
        e2e_log "FAIL" "expect failed with exit $errno"
        cat "$output_file" >> "$E2E_TEST_LOG"
        cat "$output_file"
        rm "$output_file"
        return $errno
    fi
}

teardown() {
    {
        echo ""
        echo "========================================"
        echo "Test completed: $(date -Iseconds)"
        echo "Result: ${BATS_TEST_COMPLETED:-unknown}"
        echo "========================================"
    } >> "$E2E_TEST_LOG"
}
```

### Sample Expect Script (tests/e2e/expect_helpers/complete_wizard.exp)
```tcl
#!/usr/bin/expect -f

set timeout 30
set tmpdir [lindex $argv 0]
set project_name [lindex $argv 1]

log_file -a /tmp/expect_debug.log

spawn bash -c "cd $tmpdir && newproj --interactive"

expect {
    "Welcome to newproj" {
        send "\r"
    }
    timeout {
        puts "ERROR: Timeout waiting for welcome screen"
        exit 1
    }
}

expect {
    "Project name:" {
        send "$project_name\r"
    }
    timeout {
        puts "ERROR: Timeout waiting for project name prompt"
        exit 1
    }
}

# ... continue for all screens ...

expect {
    "Project created successfully" {
        puts "SUCCESS: Wizard completed"
        exit 0
    }
    timeout {
        puts "ERROR: Timeout waiting for completion"
        exit 1
    }
}
```

## CI Integration

```yaml
# In GitHub Actions
- name: Run E2E tests
  run: |
    sudo apt-get install -y expect
    bats tests/e2e/*.bats --tap
    
- name: Upload E2E logs on failure
  if: failure()
  uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
  with:
    name: e2e-logs
    path: tests/e2e_logs/
```

## Deliverables
- [ ] tests/e2e/ directory structure
- [ ] E2E test helper with logging
- [ ] expect scripts for common flows
- [ ] At least 10 E2E test cases
- [ ] CI integration for E2E tests
- [ ] E2E log rotation (keep last 50)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All E2E tests pass: `bats tests/e2e/`
- E2E tests produce detailed logs in tests/e2e_logs/
- CI runs E2E tests and uploads logs on failure
- Happy path, cancellation, validation, and recovery all tested
- Terminal compatibility verified for xterm, screen, tmux, dumb","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T23:49:21.876618572Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T05:35:45.680472286Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T05:35:45.680472286Z","close_reason":"E2E test suite implemented with 53 test cases covering CLI mode, terminal compatibility, CI detection, navigation, and error recovery. Tests work in quick mode (CLI only) and full mode (with expect for TUI automation).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-o9dh","depends_on_id":"bd-kvob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T23:59:31.555285609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-o9dh","depends_on_id":"bd-ydax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T23:49:28.057296460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-o9q6","title":"FEATURE: Agent Commands & NTM Palette Pages","description":"Transform the Agent Commands (/learn/agent-commands) and NTM Palette (/learn/ntm-palette) pages into premium reference experiences.

**Current State:**
These are specialized command reference pages for the three AI agents and the NTM orchestration tool. They likely share patterns with the main commands page.

**Improvements Needed (both pages):**
- Match the visual language of the enhanced commands page
- Add agent-specific theming (different accent colors per agent)
- Interactive demos where applicable
- Quick-copy functionality with feedback
- Keyboard navigation
- Mobile-optimized layouts

**Agent Commands Specific:**
- Claude/Codex/Gemini should have distinct visual identities
- Show which agent is best for which use case
- Add 'Try it' suggestions
- Cross-link between agents

**NTM Palette Specific:**
- Visual representation of agent orchestration
- Show session layouts visually
- Interactive palette builder preview
- Real-world workflow examples

**Files:**
- apps/web/app/learn/agent-commands/page.tsx
- apps/web/app/learn/ntm-palette/page.tsx","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-25T05:15:04.655496Z","updated_at":"2025-12-25T05:22:53.712322Z","closed_at":"2025-12-25T05:22:53.712322Z","close_reason":"Merged into Commands Reference (7idl). @@ -298,6 +298,28 @@ EOF
     assert_success

+@test "update_nvm_node_bin_dir: picks newest executable node binary" {
+    local older_bin="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v20.11.1/bin"
+    local newer_bin="$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v99.0.0/bin"
+
+    mkdir -p "$older_bin" "$newer_bin"
+    cat > "$older_bin/node" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+echo older node
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$older_bin/node"
+    touch "$newer_bin/node"
+
+    run update_nvm_node_bin_dir
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "$older_bin"
+
+    chmod +x "$newer_bin/node"
+    run update_nvm_node_bin_dir
+    assert_success
+    assert_output "$newer_bin"
+}
